tools.title: 16" Macbook Pro, M4 Pro
tools.description: 48 GB RAM (2024). The cockpit. Claude Code, Codex, Cursor and a fleet of AI agents run wild across worktrees while I orchestrate from the driver's seat.

tools.title: 16" MacBook Pro, M2 Pro
tools.description: 32 GB RAM (2023). Where the talks get built, novels get written, and passion projects come to life. The machine that reminds me coding isn't everything.

tools.title: 14" MacBook Pro, M2 Pro
tools.description: 32 GB RAM (2023). Runs headless 24/7. Plex server, self-hosted apps, and autonomous AI agents that work while I sleep.

tools.title: 4K 32" LG UltraFine Ergo
tools.description: 32 inches of 4K real estate on an ergonomic arm. More pixels means more worktrees visible at once. The stand alone is worth it.

tools.title: Keychron K4 Wireless
tools.description: Mechanical clicks that make every keystroke feel intentional. Full-size layout, wireless freedom.

tools.title: Logitech MX Master 3
tools.description: I live in the terminal, so the mouse rarely moves. But when it does, it's this one.

tools.title: Greensoul Imperium
tools.description: Electric sit-stand desk. Because debugging posture is just as important as debugging code.

tools.title: Sennheiser HD 458BT
tools.description: Noise-cancelling companion for deep work. Podcasts in, distractions out.

tools.title: Zed
tools.description: Native speed, zero lag. When you're juggling a dozen projects across worktrees, every millisecond of editor latency matters.

tools.title: Oh My Zsh!
tools.description: Auto-cd, smart completions, and a theme that sparks joy. The shell shouldn't fight you. It should read your mind.

tools.title: Ghostty
tools.description: Blazing fast, GPU-rendered, and endlessly configurable. The terminal where the real work happens.

tools.title: dmux
tools.description: The secret weapon. Spins up parallel AI agents across worktrees, manages sessions, and turns one terminal into a command center. Built on tmux.

tools.title: CodexBar
tools.description: Real-time token burn rate, right in the menu bar. Know exactly what your agents are costing. Always.

tools.title: Paper
tools.description: Idea to mockup to code in one flow. AI-powered design that actually ships, not just pretty pictures.

tools.title: Excalidraw
tools.description: When I need to think visually: architecture diagrams, flow charts, or questionable logo concepts. Fast and delightfully rough.

tools.title: Tiles
tools.description: Keyboard-driven window tiling. 2x faster at arranging windows, 2x slower at deciding where they go.

tools.title: Notion
tools.description: Notes, projects, plans, and now my calendar too. All in one place. Notion absorbed Cron and became the single source of truth for my entire digital life.

tools.title: Spark
tools.description: Smart filters keep the inbox at zero. If Spark didn't surface it, it probably wasn't worth reading.

tools.title: Simplenote
tools.description: The digital napkin. Capture a thought on any device, forget about it forever. Blissfully simple.

tools.title: BrowserOS
tools.description: The browser I'm building. Gives every AI agent eyes to see the web. Navigate, interact, extract. The missing sense for autonomous workflows.

tools.title: Claude Code
tools.description: The go-to for focused, deep coding sessions. Understands context like a senior engineer sitting next to you.

tools.title: Codex
tools.description: Fire-and-forget. Spins up ideas, runs experiments, handles the passive work while I focus elsewhere.

tools.title: Cursor + GLM 5.1
tools.description: The experimental rig. Cursor's IDE + Z.ai's GLM for refactors, migrations, and the grunt work nobody wants to do.

tools.title: Gemini CLI
tools.description: When the task is Material Design or mobile-first UI, Gemini gets it right out of the box.

tools.title: Greptile
tools.description: AI-powered reviews for high-stakes private repos. Catches what humans miss after the third PR of the day.

tools.title: GitHub Copilot
tools.description: Handles reviews for side projects and open source. Keeps the quality bar up across everything I maintain.

tools.title: Hermes Agent
tools.description: Powered by Kimi K2.5 & GLM 5.1. A safe, sandboxed agent that never sleeps and never says no. Handles ad-hoc tasks around the clock: research, automation, whatever I throw at it.
